methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Long Wall is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Dan Piemont and Harry O'Hanley. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: four Series A companies (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M, NODA AI at $125M) and one seed-stage peer (Tenna Systems at $60M). The broader comp universe includes two public companies and eight private firms across defense and security infrastructure.

The peer-median method applies directly to Long Wall's profile. The five comparables yield a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. This method captures representative pricing at the target's stage within the defense and security sector. No other valuation triangulation methods were applicable given available data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered at $150M. This range reflects the median implied by peers while acknowledging natural dispersion within a small comparable set. Key uncertainties include product maturity relative to peers, customer traction, technology differentiation, and team track record—factors not fully captured by stage and sector alone. The wide spread between the lowest peer ($60M) and highest ($500M) underscores sensitivity to company-specific execution and defensibility.
